source: Dutch Safety Board Final Report, Oct 2015
note: 283 passengers and 15 crew. All 298 aboard were killed. No survivors. The aircraft disintegrated at 33,000 feet over eastern Ukraine.
Malaysia Airlines crew: 3 flight crew (2 pilots, 1 additional pilot) and 12 cabin crew. All were Malaysian nationals.

196 Dutch nationals and residents killed — the largest national group. Some sources cite 192–193 Dutch citizens specifically; 196 includes Dutch residents. The Netherlands bore the greatest national loss.
44 Malaysian nationals killed, including 15 crew and 29 passengers. Malaysia Airlines operated the flight; MH17 was returning to Kuala Lumpur.
27 Australian citizens killed. Australia co-sponsored UNSC Resolution 2166, was a founding JIT member, and has been a leading international advocate for MH17 accountability.
12 Indonesian nationals killed. Many were affiliated with HIV/AIDS research and were traveling to an international AIDS conference in Melbourne, Australia.
10 British nationals killed. The UK was not a JIT member but provided assistance through GCHQ intelligence sharing and bilateral cooperation with Dutch investigators.
9 victims from additional nationalities: Germany (4), Belgium (4), Philippines (3), Canada (3), New Zealand (1), and others. A total of 17 nationalities were represented among the 298 aboard MH17.
